questions.text: Which of the following is regarded as the oldest civilization in Nigeria based on archaeological evidence?
questions.explanation: The Nok Culture is famous for its unique terracotta sculptures and is dating back from 1500 BC to 200 AD, making it the oldest known iron-age civilization in Nigeria.

questions.text: The traditional socio-political organization of the pre-colonial Igbo society was characterized as:
questions.explanation: Pre-colonial Igbo society was highly decentralized and democratic, relying on village groups, titles, and elder councils rather than centralized monarchs.

questions.text: In pre-colonial administration, the Yoruba council of kingmakers is known as:
questions.explanation: The Oyomesi was the council of state and kingmakers in the old Oyo Empire, who served as a check on the powers of the Alaafin (king).

questions.text: Under the British colonial administration, the amalgamation of the Northern and Southern protectorates of Nigeria took place in what year?
questions.explanation: Lord Frederick Lugard amalgamated the Northern and Southern protectorates of Nigeria in January 1914 to form a single political entity.

questions.text: The historical trade route across the Sahara desert that linked West Africa to North Africa and Europe is called:
questions.explanation: The Trans-Saharan trade route facilitated the exchange of goods like gold, salt, and leather between West African kingdoms and North African traders.
